content.problemStatement:
Given a `N x M` matrix, print its element in zig-zag fashion, i.e., print first row from left to right, second row from right to left, third row again from left to right and so on.

For eg., for the following matrix :



Output should be: 4 6 0 1 2 8 3 1 5


Input Format

The first line of input will contain two space separated integers NNN and MMM, denoting the no. of rows and columns in the input matrix.
Next NNN lines contains MMM space separated integers, the elements of the matrix.


Output Format

Output on a single line, N‚àóMN*MN‚àóM elements of the given matrix in zig-zag fashion.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers N and M ‚Äî the number of rows and columns of the matrix. Then N lines follow, each containing M space‚Äëseparated non‚Äënegative integers representing the matrix rows.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing N¬∑M integers ‚Äì the elements of the matrix traversed in zig‚Äëzag order (first row left‚Üíright, second row right‚Üíleft, and so on). Numbers are separated by a single space and there is no trailing space at the end of the line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Row 0 (even) left‚Üíright: 4 6 0. Row 1 (odd) right‚Üíleft: 1 2 8. Row 2 (even) left‚Üíright: 3 1 5. Concatenated result matches the output.
First row left‚Üíright gives 1 2. Second row right‚Üíleft gives 4 3.

hidden.label: edge case with zeros and duplicates

solution.approach:
For each test case we read the matrix into a 2‚ÄëD array. We then iterate over rows i = 0 ‚Ä¶ N‚Äë1. If i is even (0‚Äëbased) we output the row from column 0 to M‚Äë1. If i is odd we output the row from column M‚Äë1 down to 0. While printing we keep a flag to avoid a leading space and ensure a single space between numbers. This yields the required zig‚Äëzag order.
The algorithm is straightforward linear scanning; no extra data structures are needed beyond the matrix itself.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * M) ‚Äì each element is visited exactly once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * M) for storing the matrix of each test case (can be O(1) if processed row by row, but O(N*M) is acceptable within limits).
